description: CLI wrapper for OpenAI Whisper speech-to-text transcription

create.help: Transcribe an audio or video file using OpenAI Whisper. Outputs JSON transcript with text and timestamped segments.

arguments.name: file_path
arguments.help: Path to audio/video file to transcribe

options.name: --model
options.help: Whisper model: tiny, base, small, medium, large, turbo

options.name: --language
options.help: Language code (e.g., en, es, fr, de)

create.usage_instructions: Transcribe an audio or video file. FILE_PATH is a required positional argument. Use --model/-m to select the Whisper model (tiny, base, small, medium, large, turbo; default: turbo). Use --language/-L for language code (default: en). Use --word-timestamps/-w for word-level timing. Use --output-dir/-o to save output files. Default timeout is 600s.

list.help: List existing transcript JSON files in a directory.

arguments.name: directory
arguments.help: Directory to search for transcript JSON files

list.usage_instructions: List transcript JSON files in a directory. DIRECTORY is an optional positional argument (defaults to current directory). Use --table/-t for formatted output, --filter/-f for filtering, --limit/-l to cap results (default 100).

get.help: Get details of an existing transcript file.
get.usage_instructions: Read a transcript JSON file. FILE_PATH is a required positional argument (path to the .json transcript). Use --table/-t for formatted output, --properties/-p to select fields.

models.usage_instructions: List all available Whisper models with their sizes and capabilities. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

transcripts.usage_instructions: Transcribe audio/video files and manage transcript outputs. Use create to transcribe, list to find existing transcripts, get to read one, and models to see available Whisper models.

usage_instructions: Use for transcribing audio and video files to text using OpenAI Whisper. Commands follow `whisper <group> <action>` pattern. The primary command is `transcripts create` to transcribe files. Supports multiple models (tiny, base, small, medium, large, turbo) with turbo as default. Runs locally -- no API key needed.
